pages.text: $(italic)Supplementaries'$() $(bold)Quiver$() holds your arrows so the bow can find them without filling a hotbar slot. Loaded, it feeds any bow or crossbow automatically — special arrows first, plain arrows last, so your tipped and spectral shots aren't wasted on skeletons.$(br2)Wear it in the belt/curio slot, or keep it in your inventory; either way the bow draws from it.
pages.title: Quivers

pages.text: $(bold)Where they come from.$() Two paths in Novus:$(br)$(li)$(bold)Craft an empty quiver$() and fill it yourself — the steady route. (See $(italic)EMI$().)$(li)$(bold)Take one off a bowman.$() $(novus)Skeletons$() drop a loaded quiver about $(bold)1 kill in 14$(); the icy-biome $(novus)Stray$() drops one far more often, about $(bold)1 in 7$(), and its quivers come carrying more arrows.
pages.title: Where They Come From

pages.text: $(bold)A note on the drops.$() Only kills $(italic)you$() land count — a creeper finishing a skeleton for you yields nothing, and neither does an automated farm. $(italic)Looting$() raises the odds. The drop is a shortcut to a pre-loaded quiver, not the only way to own one.
pages.title: Earned, Not Farmed
